what_it_does: A desktop-first CSS stylesheet loaded last that adapts a fixed-width desktop layout down through tablet, narrow, and mobile breakpoints by collapsing grids, trimming top-bar chrome, turning the spine/drawer into an off-canvas slide-over, and reflowing wizard/intake/welcome/workspace components.
observations: Loaded after base styles; rules only activate at ≤1140px, ≤1024px, ≤860px, ≤640px, and ≤380px.
Targets page grids by attribute selectors on their inline `grid-template-columns` values, e.g. `[style*="fr 1fr"]`.
Progressively hides top-bar elements as the viewport narrows: secondary status chips, breadcrumb, app name, and finally My Vi labels.
At ≤640px the `.nav` becomes a fixed off-canvas drawer with a transform transition and a `.nav-scrim` backdrop.
Wizard and intake side panels narrow at ≤1024px and are hidden at ≤860px.
Welcome hero, workspace headers, next-step callouts, and project/template/file heads stack vertically on small screens.
Journey bar compacts by hiding phase labels at mobile.
Uses `!important` on many collapsed grid-template-columns rules.
standouts: Intentional reliance on inline-style substring selectors to style page grids without class hooks.
Off-canvas mobile navigation with hamburger, scrim, and transform animation.
Gradual retirement of top-bar chrome across multiple breakpoints rather than a single mobile cutoff.
conventions: Desktop-first responsive approach using `max-width` media queries.
Heavy use of CSS custom properties for color, radius, and easing values.
Extensive explanatory comments documenting strategy and breakpoint rationale.
Overrides inline-authored layout with `!important` utility-style rules.
concerns: Attribute selectors depend on exact inline grid strings; any change to those inline styles would break the responsive rules.
Broad selector `.canvas__inner [style*="grid-template-columns"]` with `!important` may unintentionally collapse grids that should remain multi-column.
Duplicate declarations for hiding `.topbar__crumb` appear at both 860px and 640px breakpoints.
No visible focus-management or reduced-motion handling for the animated off-canvas drawer.
notes: This is the surgical responsive layer: it leaves the desktop design untouched and uses inline-style substring selectors to collapse complex grids as the viewport shrinks.
questions: What JavaScript toggles the `.nav-open` class on `.app`?
Are the inline `grid-template-columns` strings guaranteed to remain stable across all pages?
How is keyboard focus trapped or returned when the off-canvas nav opens and closes?
What is the intended accessible label or behavior for `.topbar__menu`?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—adapt the fixed-desktop layout down to tablet and phone while leaving desktop unchanged—matches the actual content.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to preserve the exact desktop design while minimally and declaratively degrading multi-column layouts to stacked/mobile views, avoiding changes to component markup by hooking into stable inline grid strings.
novelty: The deliberate, commented strategy of using attribute substring selectors on inline `grid-template-columns` values as the primary responsive targeting mechanism.
contribution: Provides the project's responsive adaptation layer: breakpoint-specific grid collapsing, off-canvas mobile navigation, top-bar chrome reduction, and reflow rules for wizard/intake/welcome/workspace components, intended to be loaded after base styles.
summary_for_embedding: A desktop-first responsive CSS file that uses inline-style substring selectors and max-width media queries to collapse fixed-desktop grids, trim top-bar chrome, and convert navigation to an off-canvas drawer for tablet and mobile viewports.
intention_for_embedding: To adapt a fixed-desktop counterpart UI down to narrower viewports without altering component markup or the desktop layout.
